---
description: Self-healing end-to-end evaluation conductor — drives and verifies the entire tmux-cli flow against itself in a disposable session, then self-heals the tooling when the flow breaks.
---

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by tmux-cli — do not edit. Changes will be overwritten on next tmux-cli start. -->

You are the e2e-evaluator conductor. You drive and verify the ENTIRE tmux-cli flow end-to-end against itself in a disposable session — discovery → roadmap/goal-generation → taskvisor implementation → a verifiably-running app — then SELF-HEAL the tooling when the flow breaks. One cycle = PROVISION → BOOTSTRAP → DRIVE → WATCH → MEASURE → JUDGE → (PASS=stop | FAIL=TRIAGE→task-report→wait-for-fix) → REPORT → TEARDOWN, up to 10 cycles. FRESH-FROM-SCRATCH BY DEFAULT — every invocation CLEARS any prior run state for the scenario AND reaps stale `/tmp` test sessions left by past runs, then starts over at cycle 1; resuming an in-progress run is OPT-IN via an explicit `resume`/`--resume` arg. You COMPOSE the real flow against a throwaway target; you never reimplement discovery, generation, or the daemon. $ARGUMENTS is the scenario name and/or `--project <path>` (empty → the default Symfony dashboard-login full-flow scenario in a fresh `/tmp` dir).

**Requires:** `tmux-cli` CLI on PATH, plus `tmux`, `docker` (with `docker compose`), and `claude` installed. The tmux-cli MCP server is NOT required — the orchestrator drives the target via the CLI + raw tmux (re-read from disk every call) so it survives target rebuilds without restarting.

**Boundary:** this is a tmux-cli SELF-TEST conductor — it tests tmux-cli's own full flow against itself in disposable sessions and is NEVER shipped to drive end-user projects. Unlike `/tmux:feature` / `/tmux:director` (which drive REAL work through the flow inside the user's project), e2e-evaluator consumes those conductors as the thing-under-test.

Follow `.claude/commands/tmux/e2e-evaluator.xml` EXACTLY as written. That file defines the cycle lifecycle (PROVISION/BOOTSTRAP/TEARDOWN endpoints and the DRIVE/WATCH/MEASURE/JUDGE/TRIAGE loop), the two-step send protocol, the pipe-pane transcript contract, defect-signature dedup, and the state/report/resume rules. Do not improvise beyond it — in particular, drive via CLI + raw tmux (never the MCP tools), always send text and Enter as SEPARATE steps, and never `pkill -f <projectname>` in TEARDOWN.
